heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No storms are expected.  The last storm was observed on 18 July 2026 and continued for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 2nd since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 03 July 2026 and has lasted 19 days.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 3 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is currently a heatwave.  It has been going on for 21 days and it started on 2026-07-01.  Currently this year there have been 3 heatwaves, usually there are 1.9 heatwaves per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and it has only happened 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (208°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 4.9°C) and outside temperature (down 4.2°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 18%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 21343 lux) and cloud levels (up 2298 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 19137 lux) and cloud levels (up 1480 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2300m lower than yesterday, 1480.2m lower than the past week, 825.4m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 1974m and a high of 8460m, averaging 2890m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: The sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; like a nice, warm day. Also, humidity should not be a concern and winds will be practically absent. A few light raindrops may fall, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and bright intervals likely. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
